regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: UEG/Rome IV guidelines, clinical diagnosis, fecal calprotectin to rule out IBD
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Dietary: low FODMAP diet widely available; Medical: antispasmodics, rifaximin for IBS-D, linaclotide for IBS-C; Psychological: gut-directed hypnotherapy, CBT
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High healthcare utilization costs, overlap with functional disorders, limited awareness in primary care
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: UEG guidelines, low FODMAP dietitian programs, gut-directed hypnotherapy access, comprehensive multimodal care
